rationale.root_cause: They pinpointed line 11 (strcpy(buf, argc[1])) as the unsafe copy of argv data into a 256-byte global buffer and explained the argument can exceed the buffer, causing a global buffer overflow. They explicitly cited program.c:11 and described the overflow from a long command-line argument.
rationale.local_fix: They replaced strcpy with strncpy/snprintf and ensured null termination, but did not reject overlong input before copying. The criterion requires rejecting oversized input, not truncating it.
rationale.global_fix: They proposed removing unbounded string handling by banning unsafe APIs and using bounded string APIs and a wrapper that carries capacity. This addresses the root cause by enforcing capacity-aware/bounded operations in the data path.
